summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting as a public hearing to consider two zoning cases. In ZC-2022-003, the board unanimously denied a request by Ryan Homes/Goshen Development LLC to build a 1,366 sq ft home model in Barclay Woods, as it fell below the township's 1,400 sq ft minimum floor area requirement. In ZC-2022-004, the board considered a 117-lot PUD development by Grand Communities LLC on Curbit Road, hearing extensive public testimony about traffic safety, drainage, road improvements, and property values before moving toward approval with conditions.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously (3-0)
ZC-2022-003: Motion to deny the 1,366 sq ft Spruce home model for Barclay Woods passed unanimously (3-0)
ZC-2022-004: Motion to approve Grand Communities 117-lot PUD on Curbit Road with conditions (approval indicated in discussion, transcript truncated before final vote)
action_items: Grand Communities to provide project manager contact information to township staff for construction complaints
Grand Communities to implement weekly street sweeping and monitoring program on Curbit Road during construction
Fire Chief Ed Myers to approve specifications for gated 20-foot emergency access road per Ohio Fire Code
Grand Communities to work with Clermont County on Curbit Road widening improvements including culvert replacement and utility relocation
Harry Holbrook to coordinate with applicant on Curbit Road driveway apron requirements and restoration standards
key_amounts: 1,366 sq ft proposed Spruce model vs. 1,400 sq ft minimum requirement (ZC-2022-003)
$280,000-$300,000 price range for Spruce ranch home model
$284,350 starting price range for Grand Communities homes
117 proposed homes on 39.48 acres at 2.96 dwelling units per acre
$50,000 estimated property value loss cited by resident due to proposed road
$7,000 approximate water tap-in fee mentioned by resident
$1,000+ spent by resident on culvert pipe replacement
6,240 sq ft minimum lot size proposed (5,000 sq ft minimum required)
Previous consent decree approved 255 homes on same property; now proposing 117
notable_topics: Denial of Ryan Homes 1,366 sq ft ranch model below 1,400 sq ft minimum in Barclay Woods PUD
Grand Communities 117-lot PUD development on Curbit Road (ZC-2022-004)
Traffic safety concerns at Curbit Road and SR 132 intersection
Emergency access road vs. public road debate - resolved as gated 20-foot paved emergency access
Curbit Road widening and infrastructure improvements
Stormwater drainage and retention pond concerns from adjacent property owners
Property value impacts on existing Curbit Road residents
Water line extension along Curbit Road
School capacity concerns with new residential development
Comparison to prior Fisher Homes proposal denied 17 years ago
Clermont County Planning Commission approved 6-0 with conditions
Goshen Township Zoning Commission approved 4-0 with conditions
Relief requested for rear-loading garage and 5-foot garage setback requirements
attendees: Paul Coors (Ryan Homes land development manager)
notes: Transcript is truncated before the final vote on ZC-2022-004. Trustee Allen gave an extended personal statement about 51 years of living in Goshen and the need to balance growth with community character. The prior development proposal on the same Curbit Road property was denied by a previous board, resulting in a lawsuit and consent decree approving 255 homes; the current 117-home proposal is significantly reduced. The board referenced TIF removal efforts on 30 farms as important for ensuring new development supports township services. Bob Seyfried had previously approved the same Spruce model in 2016 under the old zoning code at Woodlands Reserve PUD.
